basic_details.job_title: DGQA Technician Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 15 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Directorate General of Quality Assurance (DGQA), Ministry of Defence
basic_details.short_information: The Directorate General of Quality Assurance (DGQA), Department of Defence Production, Ministry of Defence has invited applications for Direct Recruitment of 15 Technician (Semi-Skilled), Group 'C', Non-Ministerial, Industrial posts under Advertisement No. CQA(HV)/2026/Tech(SS). The posts are spread across three establishments in Tamil Nadu and Telangana, covering the trades of Fitter Auto, Electrician Mate, and Labourer. Applications must be submitted offline by Speed Post.

important_dates.raw: This notification was carried in the Employment News issue dated 18–24 July 2026. Candidates should confirm the exact publication date and calculate the precise closing date accordingly, or check the DGQA/CQA(HV) office for confirmation, since the notification itself does not state a specific closing date.

age_limit.details: Candidates must be between 18 and 27 years of age as on the application closing date. Date of birth must be as recorded in the 10th/Matriculation Certificate, or a Birth Certificate issued by a Competent Authority, or the e-PPO for Ex-servicemen. Aadhaar Card will not be accepted as proof of date of birth. | Candidature in a reserved category remains provisional until the veracity of supporting documents is confirmed by the appointing authority. SC/ST/EWS/PwBD/ESM candidates must enclose the latest copy of a caste certificate or other relevant certificate issued by a competent authority (District Magistrate, Additional District Magistrate, Collector, Deputy Commissioner, or equivalent officer not below the rank of First Class Stipendiary Magistrate; or a Revenue Officer not below the rank of Tehsildar).

qualification.details: A person shall not be eligible for appointment if they have entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a living spouse, or if they, having a living spouse, have entered into or contracted a marriage with any other person - unless the Government is satisfied that such marriage is permissible under the personal law applicable and grants an exemption. Candidates who are convicted or currently under trial for association with criminal offences are also disqualified.

Senior Quality Assurance Establishment (Vehicles), DGQA Complex

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be placed in Pay Matrix Level-1 (₹18,000–56,900) as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix. Recruited candidates will also be entitled to other benefits including dearness allowance, house rent allowance, transport allowance, children education allowance, leave travel concession, medical facilities, CSD facilities, and other allowances/advances as per extant Government of India rules. Candidates will be covered under the National Pension Scheme (NPS)/Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) as per extant rules.

selection_process: Selection consists of a Written Test followed by a Trade Test for shortlisted candidates.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted only by Speed Post - online or email applications are not accepted.
Fill the application in the prescribed format as per Appendix 'A', typed in English on A4-size paper in block letters.
Fill the Admit Card as per Appendix 'B', in duplicate.
Attach a self-addressed envelope (30 cm x 12 cm) affixed with a ₹55 postage stamp for sending the admit card.
Attach three recent passport-size photographs - one affixed on the application form and two self-signed photographs, one each on the admit card copies.
Attach all required self-attested supporting documents as listed above.
Clearly superscribe the envelope as: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF TECHNICIAN (SEMI SKILLED) TRADE: ___, ESTABLISHMENT CODE: ___".
Send the complete application by Speed Post to: The Controller, Controllerate of Quality Assurance (Heavy Vehicles), Post Box No. 20, Avadi, Chennai-600054.

Candidates working in Government Service or a Public Sector Undertaking must forward their application through proper channel, along with a certificate from their Head of Office/Establishment confirming that no vigilance or disciplinary case is pending or contemplated against them, and that they have no objection to their release if selected. Applications not accompanied by a "No Objection Certificate" will be summarily rejected without further communication.
important_instructions: Candidates should ensure they fulfil all eligibility conditions before applying; admission to all stages of the exam is provisional and subject to satisfying eligibility criteria on verification. Applications received after the due date, even due to postal delay, will be rejected summarily with no correspondence entertained. Applications not in A4 size, incomplete, unsigned, undated, or improperly filled will be rejected. Furnishing false, inaccurate, tampered, or dubious information will lead to disqualification. More than one application submitted by the same candidate for the same post will lead to rejection of all such applications. Canvassing in any form, or bringing any political or other influence, will result in disqualification. No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the written test or trade test; candidates must make their own arrangements for lodging/boarding. The recruitment process can be cancelled, postponed, suspended, or terminated without notice at any stage. Misrepresentation, hiding, or falsification of facts detected at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature without notice. Appointment of provisionally selected candidates is strictly based on verification of documents submitted, medical examination report, and character/antecedent verification.

rows.details: Directorate General of Quality Assurance (DGQA), Ministry of Defence

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: This notification was carried in the Employment News issue dated 18–24 July 2026. Candidates should confirm the exact publication date and calculate the precise closing date accordingly, or check the DGQA/CQA(HV) office for confirmation, since the notification itself does not state a specific closing date.

content_sections.heading: DGQA Technician Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 15 vacancies have been notified across three establishments and three trades. UR = Unreserved, SC = Scheduled Caste, ST = Scheduled Tribe, EWS = Economically Weaker Section, ESM = Ex-Serviceman. Selected candidates will be initially appointed on probation for two years at Avadi (Chennai, Tamil Nadu), Yeddumailaram (Telangana), or Meenambakkam (Chennai, Tamil Nadu), and are liable to serve anywhere within India, including field and remote locations, as per extant Government of India rules.

rows.establishment_name: Controllerate of Quality Assurance (Infantry Combat Vehicles)
Senior Quality Assurance Establishment (Vehicles), DGQA Complex

content_sections.heading: Terms of Posting
content_sections.text: Each candidate can submit only one application, indicating a specific trade and specific posting station. If a candidate submits more than one application, all applications submitted by that candidate will be summarily rejected without assigning any cause. No change of post or posting station will be entertained after submission. However, DGQA reserves the right to post a candidate anywhere in India based on organisational requirement or order of merit.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates possessing any additional qualification, or pursuing one as on the application closing date, may mention this in the application form. However, only the essential ITI qualification is required for the post; any higher qualification possessed must still be disclosed.

rows.required_qualification: ITI pass certificate in Automobile/Motor Vehicle Mechanic/Diesel Mechanic, or its equivalent Defence Services Tradesman course approved by the Central or State Government
ITI pass certificate in Electrician trade, or its equivalent Defence Services Tradesman course approved by the Central or State Government
ITI pass certificate in any trade, or completion of a Defence Services Tradesman course approved by the Central or State Government

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must be between 18 and 27 years of age as on the application closing date. Date of birth must be as recorded in the 10th/Matriculation Certificate, or a Birth Certificate issued by a Competent Authority, or the e-PPO for Ex-servicemen. Aadhaar Card will not be accepted as proof of date of birth. Candidature in a reserved category remains provisional until the veracity of supporting documents is confirmed by the appointing authority. SC/ST/EWS/PwBD/ESM candidates must enclose the latest copy of a caste certificate or other relevant certificate issued by a competent authority (District Magistrate, Additional District Magistrate, Collector, Deputy Commissioner, or equivalent officer not below the rank of First Class Stipendiary Magistrate; or a Revenue Officer not below the rank of Tehsildar).

rows.age_relaxation_beyond_upper_limit: 5 years (available only for vacancies reserved for SC/ST)
3 years (available only for vacancies reserved for OBC)
3 years, after deduction of military service rendered from actual age as on closing date
6 years (3+3), after deduction of military service rendered
8 years (3+5), after deduction of military service rendered

rows.category: Departmental candidates with minimum 3 years continuous service

content_sections.heading: Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Selected candidates will be placed in Pay Matrix Level-1 (₹18,000–56,900) as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix. Recruited candidates will also be entitled to other benefits including dearness allowance, house rent allowance, transport allowance, children education allowance, leave travel concession, medical facilities, CSD facilities, and other allowances/advances as per extant Government of India rules. Candidates will be covered under the National Pension Scheme (NPS)/Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) as per extant rules.

content_sections.heading: Disqualification
content_sections.text: A person shall not be eligible for appointment if they have entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a living spouse, or if they, having a living spouse, have entered into or contracted a marriage with any other person - unless the Government is satisfied that such marriage is permissible under the personal law applicable and grants an exemption. Candidates who are convicted or currently under trial for association with criminal offences are also disqualified.

content_sections.heading: Medical Fitness
content_sections.text: Candidates must be in good mental and physical health, free from any physical deformity that may interfere with efficient discharge of duties. Provisionally shortlisted candidates must undergo a medical examination at a Government hospital before an appointment offer is issued; candidates found unfit will not be offered appointment. Candidates diagnosed with night blindness or colour blindness will be declared medically unfit and will not be considered for selection or appointment at any stage, irrespective of their performance in the recruitment process. The decision of the designated Medical Board/Medical Officer on visual defects is final and binding.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection consists of a Written Test followed by a Trade Test for shortlisted candidates. The written test is objective, multiple-choice type, with no negative marking for wrong answers. It is conducted in a single session - either forenoon or afternoon. Question paper syllabus is based on the Tenth Class/Matriculation level of a Central or State Board. Parts (i), (ii), and (iv) will be set in both English and Hindi. Part (iii) will be in English only. Candidates shortlisted based on written test marks and category of reservation will be called for a Trade Test worth 100 marks. The minimum qualifying mark in the Trade Test is 40. After both the written test and trade test are completed, a merit list will be prepared based on trade and establishment. In case two or more candidates score equal marks (trade-cum-station wise), ties will be resolved in the following order: Date of birth - older candidates placed higher. Higher marks in Numerical Aptitude, then Reasoning, then General Awareness. Selected candidates will be informed of their selection only after the process is complete; no correspondence will be entertained in this regard. Success in the examination does not confer a right to appointment unless the Government, after due enquiry, is satisfied that the candidate is suitable in all respects for the post.

content_sections.heading: Location and Date of Examination
content_sections.text: The examination will be conducted in Avadi, Chennai, Tamil Nadu. The exact date, time, and venue will be mentioned in the Admit Card.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Self-attested copies of educational qualification/technical qualification certificates Experience certificate, if applicable Self-attested copy of Date of Birth certificate (10th/Matriculation certificate or Birth Certificate) Self-attested copy of Caste certificate (OBC/SC/ST), if applicable Self-attested copy of EWS certificate, if applicable Self-attested copy of e-PPO/Discharge Certificate for Ex-servicemen, if applicable Self-attested copy of Aadhaar card No Objection Certificate (NOC) from current employer, if applicable Two copies of duly filled Admit Card (Appendix 'B') Signed instruction sheet for examination (Appendix 'C') Self-addressed envelope (30 cm x 12 cm) affixed with postage stamp of ₹55 Three recent passport-size photographs

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted only by Speed Post - online or email applications are not accepted. Fill the application in the prescribed format as per Appendix 'A', typed in English on A4-size paper in block letters. Fill the Admit Card as per Appendix 'B', in duplicate. Attach a self-addressed envelope (30 cm x 12 cm) affixed with a ₹55 postage stamp for sending the admit card. Attach three recent passport-size photographs - one affixed on the application form and two self-signed photographs, one each on the admit card copies. Attach all required self-attested supporting documents as listed above. Clearly superscribe the envelope as: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF TECHNICIAN (SEMI SKILLED) TRADE: ___, ESTABLISHMENT CODE: ___". Send the complete application by Speed Post to: The Controller, Controllerate of Quality Assurance (Heavy Vehicles), Post Box No. 20, Avadi, Chennai-600054. Candidates working in Government Service or a Public Sector Undertaking must forward their application through proper channel, along with a certificate from their Head of Office/Establishment confirming that no vigilance or disciplinary case is pending or contemplated against them, and that they have no objection to their release if selected. Applications not accompanied by a "No Objection Certificate" will be summarily rejected without further communication.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates should ensure they fulfil all eligibility conditions before applying; admission to all stages of the exam is provisional and subject to satisfying eligibility criteria on verification. Applications received after the due date, even due to postal delay, will be rejected summarily with no correspondence entertained. Applications not in A4 size, incomplete, unsigned, undated, or improperly filled will be rejected. Furnishing false, inaccurate, tampered, or dubious information will lead to disqualification. More than one application submitted by the same candidate for the same post will lead to rejection of all such applications. Canvassing in any form, or bringing any political or other influence, will result in disqualification. No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the written test or trade test; candidates must make their own arrangements for lodging/boarding. The recruitment process can be cancelled, postponed, suspended, or terminated without notice at any stage. Misrepresentation, hiding, or falsification of facts detected at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature without notice. Appointment of provisionally selected candidates is strictly based on verification of documents submitted, medical examination report, and character/antecedent verification.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in DGQA Technician (Semi-Skilled) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 15 total vacancies across the trades of Fitter Auto, Electrician Mate, and Labourer, spread over three establishments: CH-054 (Avadi, Chennai), YM-205 (Yeddumailaram, Telangana), and CH-061 (Nanganallur, Chennai).

faq.question: Q2. What is the qualification required for DGQA Technician (Semi-Skilled)?
faq.answer: An ITI pass certificate in the specified trade (or its equivalent Defence Services Tradesman course) is required — the specific trade depends on the post applied for (Fitter Auto, Electrician Mate, or Labourer).

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for DGQA Technician recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 27 years old as on the application closing date, with age relaxation available for SC/ST, OBC, Ex-servicemen, and departmental candidates as per the notification.

faq.question: Q4. Is there a written exam for this post?
faq.answer: Yes. Selection involves an objective-type Written Test with no negative marking, followed by a Trade Test (100 marks, minimum qualifying marks 40) for candidates shortlisted from the written test.

faq.question: Q5. Can I apply online for DGQA Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. This recruitment accepts only offline applications sent by Speed Post to the Controller, CQA(HV), Avadi, Chennai. Applications through any other mode will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q6. Is Aadhaar card accepted as proof of date of birth?
faq.answer: No. The notification specifically states that the Aadhaar card will not be accepted as proof of date of birth; only the 10th/Matriculation certificate, Birth Certificate, or e-PPO (for Ex-servicemen) will be accepted.
